title: AEP Lineage Orchestration Plan
description: Defines the AI-proposed Source > Connection > Dataflow deployment plan. The AI automates the construction of AEP lineage based on declared intent. The architect approves the plan, not the individual steps.

lineageHierarchy.description: The three-tier AEP architecture this plan operates within

source.definition: The physical origin of the data. A CRM, database, file store, or API endpoint.
source.aiRole: Global Scan — AI identifies all available sources before a connection is touched

connection.definition: The authenticated bridge between the source and AEP. Account or API credentials.
connection.aiRole: Credential Mapping — AI identifies which existing connections can be reused or flags that a new one is required

dataflow.definition: The specific schedule and field mapping for a dataset flowing into AEP.
dataflow.aiRole: Predictive Flow Construction — AI proposes the exact number of dataflows needed to fulfill the stated intent

proposedLineage.description: The full Source > Connection > Dataflow tree proposed by the AI to fulfill the declared intent

reused.description: Whether AI identified an existing connection that satisfies this source

dataflows.description: One or more dataflows the AI proposes to construct from this connection

reasoning.description: Why this dataflow is required for the stated intent

outcomeSimulation.description: Preview of the merged Unified Profile record state after all proposed dataflows are activated. No data has moved yet.

metadata.sprintContext: Data Mapping with Intent — POC Day 2
metadata.paradigm: Automated Data Orchestration. Human as approver, not operator.
metadata.note: This schema operates within and automates the AEP Source > Connection > Dataflow lineage model. It does not replace it.
